title: The Brain Doctor Who Found Our Words
story: A curious boy named Paul Broca was born in France in 1824. He grew up to become a brain surgeon who made an amazing discovery. Broca found the part of our brain that helps us speak! His work on June 28 showed where words come from in our heads. This special area is now called 'Broca's area' in his honor.

title: The Lightning Bolt from Barletta
story: Zoom! Pietro Mennea raced into the world on June 28, 1952, in Barletta, Italy. This speedy baby grew up to become a lightning-fast runner. Mennea dashed his way to fame, setting an incredible world record in the 200-meter sprint. His blazing speed earned him the nickname 'The Arrow of the South' in his home country.

title: Racing Legend Born in the Mountains
story: Vroom! The roar of engines echoed through the North Carolina mountains. On June 28, 1931, a future racing star was born. Junior Johnson entered the world in Wilkes County, destined for NASCAR greatness. He'd go on to win the famous Daytona 500 in 1960 and earn a spot in the NASCAR Hall of Fame. Johnson's skill behind the wheel and as a team owner left a lasting mark on the sport.
activity_question: What famous race did Junior Johnson win?
follow_up_question: Why do you think NASCAR racing is so popular in the southern United States?
